items.title: Greek LNG Buyer Says Long-Term US Deals Getting More Difficult
items.summary: The boss of Greece’s Atlantic SEE LNG Trade said it’s becoming more difficult to secure long-term liquefied natural gas deals with US suppliers, after the Iran war upended the global market.

items.title: MFS Collapse Triggers Probe Into Failed UK Lender’s Auditors
items.summary: The UK’s audit watchdog is probing accountancy firms linked to Market Financial Solutions Ltd., the mortgage lender backed by multiple Wall Street banks that collapsed in February.

items.summary: Insight with Haslinda Amin, a daily news program featuring in-depth, high-profile interviews and analysis to give viewers the complete picture on the stories that matter. The show features prominent leaders spanning the worlds of business, finance, politics and culture. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: ECB Set to Raise Rates for First Time Since 2023
items.summary: The European Central Bank is set to raise interest rates for the first time since 2023, judging it can no longer ignore the upswing in inflation caused by the Iran war. Bloomberg's Oliver Crook is in Frankfurt with a preview of today's decision. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: US Stock Futures Rise as CPI Eases Rate-Hike Fears
items.summary: US equity futures advanced after Wednesday’s inflation print calmed investor fears of Federal Reserve interest-rate hikes, while a swift end to renewed American strikes on Iran kept oil prices in check.

items.title: Indo Rate Hike Signals Resolve to Ease Angst: Robeco
items.summary: Robeco's Thu Ha Chow sees Indonesia’s out-of-cycle interest rate hike this week as reflecting policymakers’ willingness to address investor concerns. Speaking on The China Show, Chow added that while Indonesia is at the beginning of a turnaround story, it’s not quite there yet. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Iran’s ‘unity of theatres’ strategy exposes US-Israel rift
items.summary:
The rapid escalation in hostilities between Iran and the US-Israel alliance since Sunday is being driven by Tehran’s growing confidence that it can arise like a mythical phoenix from the ashes of war, strategically stronger than ever in the Middle East.
To achieve this objective, Tehran has enacted a tactical shift in its “unity of the theatres” strategy involving its Axis of Resistance allies in
